attack_surface.exposure: Every filesystem touch from action handlers now flows through `path.@this.Authorize` → AuthGate. AuthGate's auto-grant fast path is `IsInRoot()` (textual StartsWith of `_absolutePath` against the app root).
attack_surface.trust_boundary: Any caller producing a `path` whose `_absolutePath` textually prefix-matches the app root — including paths whose actual filesystem target lies outside root via `..` segments left in the string.
mitigations: AuthGate(verb) is required by every IO verb on FilePath; PLNG002 at error severity prevents handlers from bypassing path.@this entirely.
gaps: IsInRoot does not canonicalize `..` segments before the prefix check; ValidatePath skips `Path.GetFullPath` normalization when the input lexically prefix-matches rootAbsolutePath.

attack_surface.exposure: New Combine/WithName/InFolder/Parent verbs on FilePath build new FilePath objects via `System.IO.Path.Combine` without re-normalization.
attack_surface.trust_boundary: Caller supplies the child/folder/name string. Path.Combine with an absolute child overrides parent (well-known); Path.Combine with `..` does not normalize.
mitigations: Derivation produces a Path object only — no IO. All IO must go through Operations, which gate.
gaps: Same root-cause as IsInRoot: derived paths carry literal `..` segments; if the resulting `_absolutePath` happens to textually prefix-match root, AuthGate auto-grants. Not a finding on its own — same chain as F1.

attack_surface.exposure: `path.LoadAssemblyAsync()` is the only `Assembly.LoadFrom` site in the codebase (verified by grep). Gated by `Verb { Execute }`, distinct from Read.
attack_surface.trust_boundary: An actor wanting to load a DLL must have an Execute grant — Read grant does NOT cover Execute.
mitigations: Verb.AllowAll() includes Execute, but AllowAll is test-only. Production grants flow through `BuildRequest(actor, verb)` with the narrow requested verb.

attack_surface.exposure: `PathJsonConverter` reads a string off the wire and resolves it via `@this.Resolve(raw, context)` → `Scheme.From` → `file.Resolve` → ValidatePath. Used by per-Actor Json/Plang channel serializers when Context is wired; used by the global Conversion fallback (no Context) which produces stub paths that explode on Authorize.
attack_surface.trust_boundary: Anything inbound on a channel whose payload deserializes to a `Data<path>` field — including a malicious .pr arriving over the wire, a channel-driven action invocation with a path parameter, etc.
mitigations: Context-bound converter routes through Resolve; stub paths (no-Context) explode at Authorize.
gaps: The same `..`-segment-survives-IsInRoot bypass (F1) is reachable here too — a wire path `"subdir/../../etc/passwd"` deserialized in a goal-Context-bound JsonConverter triggers file.Resolve's goal-relative branch and lands as an Absolute that textually prefix-matches root.

attack_surface.exposure: `FilePath.Exists` and `FilePath.Size` (the synchronous `[LlmBuilder]`-tagged properties on file/this.cs:27,31) call `System.IO.File.Exists` / `new FileInfo(...).Length` directly without AuthGate.
attack_surface.trust_boundary: These are template/LLM-builder surfaces. Calling them at runtime gives existence/size leaks for any path the caller has the object for.
mitigations: Existence probing via `path.AsBooleanAsync()` / `ExistsAsync()` IS gated; the async path is what the truthiness pipeline uses.
gaps: Sync `Exists`/`Size` are info-disclosure side channels under non-user threat models (sub-actor probing) — but under PLang's user-sovereign model, an LLM-builder template that reads `%path.Exists%` is user-authored. Not flagging unless reachable from untrusted input — and no use sites in production handlers reach them. Standing watch.

findings.vector: IsInRoot()'s auto-grant fast path uses a textual `StartsWith` of `_absolutePath` against the app root, with no `..`-segment normalization. When `file.Resolve` combines a relative path containing `..` with a goal-anchored runtimeDir (or goalPath.Parent) that lives inside root, the resulting absolute string textually prefix-matches root — IsInRoot returns true — AuthGate auto-grants — and `System.IO.File.ReadAllBytesAsync` resolves the `..` segments at the OS layer and reads outside root with no actor prompt and no grant lookup.
findings.preconditions: A `path` whose `_absolutePath` contains unresolved `..` segments AND textually prefix-matches `app.AbsolutePath`. The natural production source is `file.Resolve` for a relative rawPath when the active Goal has a `LoadedFromPrPath` inside root (which is every goal loaded from a .pr in normal operation). Reachable from: (a) PLang code reading `%input%` where input comes from external wire, (b) wire-deserialized `Data<path>` fields, (c) handler parameters with relative-path raw values.
findings.impact: Arbitrary file read (and write/delete/execute, by symmetry — every verb auto-grants on IsInRoot=true) outside the actor's permission scope, without any prompt or grant lookup. Breaks the soundness of the entire path-permission model: sub-actor scoping is silently bypassed; promised security boundary that this whole branch was built to enforce is paper. Reproduces in ~5s as a single PLang.Tests file (mutation test attached below).
findings.exploit_sketch:
Reproduced via PLang.Tests mutation test (run + revert). Test stages a SECRET-OUTSIDE.txt one directory above the engine's rootAbsolutePath, sets up a Goal with `LoadedFromPrPath = <root>/subdir/.build/probe.fake`, sets ctx.Goal, then calls `path.@this.Resolve("../../SECRET-OUTSIDE.txt", ctx).ReadText()`. Test output:
```
Literal Absolute: /workspace/plang/PLang.Tests/bin/Debug/net10.0/subdir/../../SECRET-OUTSIDE.txt
Starts with fixturesDir? True
Success: True
Value: if-you-can-read-me-the-gate-was-bypassed-<uuid>
```
No prompt fired; the marker file was read in full.
findings.proposed_fix:
Canonicalize before the IsInRoot prefix check OR canonicalize at FilePath construction. Two acceptable shapes:
  (a) In `app.types.path.@this.IsUnder` (the helper used by `IsInRoot`), call `System.IO.Path.GetFullPath(Absolute)` once and `StartsWith` against the canonical form. This is the minimal surgical fix.
  (b) Canonicalize in `file.Resolve` after the goal-relative `Path.Combine` and before constructing the FilePath: wrap the result in `Path.GetFullPath(resolved)` unconditionally. This also fixes the `Equals/GetHashCode/Relative` family that all use `_absolutePath` directly — they currently compare paths-with-dotdot byte-wise to paths-without-dotdot as distinct keys, which is its own subtle bug (collision/non-collision on the same physical file).

Fix (b) is preferred — it makes `_absolutePath` mean what its name says (an absolute, canonical OS path). Add a regression test of the mutation shape (LoadedFromPrPath under root + `..` rawPath + assert prompt or denial). The N3 footgun from codeanalyzer (implicit `string→path` operator) becomes meaningfully safer too: the stub-path constructor on the bare ctor would also canonicalize.

ALSO check `Path.Combine`-based FilePath derivation verbs (Combine, WithName, InFolder, Parent in `file/this.Derivation.cs`) — they each `new @this(combinedString, Context)` without normalization. The base ctor should canonicalize so derivation can't smuggle `..` segments back in either.

findings.vector: `PLang/app/modules/MarkdownTeaching.cs` performs 8 ungated `System.IO` reaches (`Directory.Exists/EnumerateDirectories/EnumerateFiles`, `File.Exists/ReadAllText` at lines 36, 73, 75, 82, 113-114, 125-126). The file is whole-file exempted by the PLNG002 analyzer (`Plng002.cs::IsScannedFile`, line 81). The loader receives a raw `string root` parameter and walks it without ever constructing a `path.@this`.
findings.preconditions: Today the root parameter comes exclusively from `Modules.ResolveMarkdownTeachingRoot()` which hardcodes `path.Resolve("/system/modules", …)`. The public override `Modules.MarkdownTeachingRoot` is settable from C# but is only assigned by tests. So today's reach is constrained to the OS-shipped teaching folder.
findings.impact: Latent. If anyone ever wires `MarkdownTeachingRoot` from config / env / hot-reload settings / a wire surface, the loader silently enumerates and reads any directory the caller names — no AuthGate, no permission lookup. The whole-file PLNG002 exemption guarantees the analyzer will never warn about it. Same shape as the standing-finding callback-signing-bypass: a permissive auth gate sitting unused until a consumer activates it.
findings.exploit_sketch: Theoretical only — requires future C# change to set MarkdownTeachingRoot from untrusted input. The analyzer wouldn't catch the addition; production safety would silently depend on the new caller's discipline.
findings.proposed_fix: Lift `MarkdownTeaching` IO to the `path.@this` verb surface — take `path.@this` instead of `string root`, call `List(pattern, recursive)` for the directory walk and `ReadText()` for content. The gate fires inside; the exemption disappears. The 'bootstrap timing' rationale in `Plng002.cs:78-80` doesn't actually hold — `Modules.Describe()` runs after App construction. Cost is sync→async migration of the loader and `ScanOrphans`/`Load` callers, which is the same cost every other handler ate in this branch.

findings.vector: PLNG002 whole-file exempts `PLang/app/this.cs` (`Plng002.cs:87`). The inline `#pragma warning disable PLNG002` brackets only `OsAbsolutePath` (lines 83-86), but the analyzer doesn't honor source pragmas at all (per the comment at lines 85-86) — the actual exemption is whole-file via `IsScannedFile`. The pragma's apparent scope misleads readers about what's actually exempted.
findings.preconditions: None today. The only System.IO reach in `app/this.cs` is the OsAbsolutePath line, which is pure path-arithmetic (`Path.GetFullPath(Path.Combine(...))`).
findings.impact: Latent. A future engineer adding any System.IO call anywhere in `app/this.cs` would silently pass the gate. Reader confusion is the active impact today: the narrow-looking pragma masks a whole-file backdoor.
findings.proposed_fix: Ingi's proposal — introduce a `PathHelper` static class containing pure path-string-math forwarders (`PathHelper.Combine => Path.Combine`, `PathHelper.GetFullPath => Path.GetFullPath`, …). The analyzer allowlist becomes 'type `PathHelper`' instead of a string-name list of `Path` members. Rewrite `OsAbsolutePath` as `PathHelper.GetFullPath(PathHelper.Combine(AppContext.BaseDirectory, "os"))`. Drop the inline `#pragma` and the whole-file `IsScannedFile` exemption for `app/this.cs`. Reviewers spot any future `PathHelper.<IO-shaped name>` (e.g. `ReadAllText`) at the type's source, not by re-reading the analyzer allowlist. Keep `PathHelper` strictly non-IO — no `File`/`Directory`/`FileInfo` forwarders; those still belong on the `path.@this` verb surface where AuthGate lives.

summary: The branch's stated goal — handlers must not bypass AuthGate — is achieved cleanly. PLNG002 at error severity locks the door at the syntactic layer; the typed path surface routes every disk touch through AuthGate. But the gate itself has a soundness flaw: `IsInRoot` uses textual `StartsWith` of an un-canonicalized `_absolutePath` against the app root, and `ValidatePath` skips `Path.GetFullPath` normalization when the input already lexically prefix-matches root. Result: a relative path containing `..` resolved against a goal-anchored directory inside root produces an `Absolute` that textually starts with root, auto-grants AuthGate, then OS-resolves outside root. Confirmed via mutation test — file outside root read in 5 seconds with no prompt. This bypass pre-existed the branch (the same flaw would have lived in `PLangFileSystem.ValidatePath` before), but it was *masked* while action handlers were bypassing AuthGate entirely. Now that the gate is the single chokepoint, the chokepoint's hole becomes the only thing that matters. Verdict: FAIL — the branch built the gate they promised, but the gate has a hole exactly the size of `..`.
